title: US-001: Add CPU information output
description:
As a developer, I want to run main.py --cpu so that I can see CPU details in the terminal.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --cpu flag is accepted by the CLI without error
- [ ] Output includes: number of logical CPU cores, architecture (e.g. x86_64, arm64), and current CPU usage percentage
- [ ] Output is human-readable plain text (e.g. CPU Cores: 8)
- [ ] Running with --cpu shows only CPU section
- [ ] Pytest tests cover the CPU data-gathering function
- [ ] python -m pytest passes
- [ ] Manually run the CLI and confirm output looks correct in a terminal

title: System Info CLI Enhancement
description: Extend main.py CLI to expose CPU, memory, and disk info with selective flags in plain text output.

title: US-001: Display Python version via default commds
description:
Acceptance Criteria:
- Running `python main.py` prints the Python version (e.g. `Python 3.14.2`)
- Output is plain text, human-readable
- Uses `sys.version_info` for structured version access
- Output includes major, minor, and micro version numbers
how now
Quality Gate: python -m pytestTesting the quality gate

title: Python CLI Tool -- System Info
description: Lightweight Python CLI tool that displays Python version and environment info in the terminal. External ref: prd:./tasks/prd-python-cli-tool-system-info.md

title: US-002: Add memory/RAM information output
description:
As a developer, I want to run main.py --memory so that I can see RAM stats in the terminal.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --memory flag is accepted by the CLI without error
- [ ] Output includes: total RAM, used RAM, available RAM (in human-readable units, e.g. GB)
- [ ] Output is human-readable plain text (e.g. Memory Total: 16.0 GB)
- [ ] Running with --memory shows only memory section
- [ ] Pytest tests cover the memory data-gathering function
- [ ] python -m pytest passes
- [ ] Manually run the CLI and confirm output looks correct in a terminal
close_reason: Implemented --memory flag with get_memory_info(), all 14 pytest tests pass

title: US-002: Add --version flag
description:
Acceptance Criteria:
- `python main.py --version` prints only the version string (e.g. `3.14.2`)
- No extra labels or formatting — just the version number
- Uses `argparse` to handle the flag
- Exits with code 0 on success

Quality Gate: python -m pytest

title: US-003: Add disk usage information output
description:
As a developer, I want to run main.py --disk so that I can see disk partition info and free space in the terminal.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --disk flag is accepted by the CLI without error
- [ ] Output includes: each mounted partition, its total size, used space, and free space (in human-readable units)
- [ ] Output is human-readable plain text
- [ ] Running with --disk shows only disk section
- [ ] Pytest tests cover the disk data-gathering function
- [ ] python -m pytest passes
- [ ] Manually run the CLI and confirm output looks correct in a terminal

title: US-003: Add --all flag for full info display
description:
Acceptance Criteria:
- `python main.py --all` displays: Python version, version info tuple, and platform string
- Output uses `colorama` to colorize labels (label in cyan, value in white)
- Falls back to plain text if `colorama` is not installed
- Each piece of info is on its own labeled line

Quality Gate: python -m pytest

title: US-005: Support combining multiple flags
description:
As a developer, I want to combine flags (e.g. main.py --cpu --memory) so that I can request exactly the categories I need.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Any combination of --cpu, --memory, --disk flags works without error
- [ ] Only the requested sections are shown in output
- [ ] Order of output matches order of flags or is consistent (document which)
- [ ] Pytest tests cover at least two multi-flag combinations
- [ ] python -m pytest passes
- [ ] Manually run the CLI and confirm output looks correct in a terminal

title: US-004: Default (no flags) shows all categories
description:
As a developer, I want running main.py with no flags to display all system info categories so that I get a full overview quickly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Running main.py with no flags outputs CPU, memory, and disk sections
- [ ] Output is clearly sectioned (e.g. headers per category)
- [ ] Existing output from the current tool is preserved or cleanly integrated
- [ ] Pytest tests cover the default (no-flag) execution path
- [ ] python -m pytest passes
- [ ] Manually run the CLI and confirm output looks correct in a terminal

title: US-005: Write unit tests for CLI behavior
description:
Acceptance Criteria:
- Test file exists at `tests/test_main.py`
- Tests verify `--version` returns a valid semver string (e.g. matches `\d+\.\d+\.\d+`)
- Tests verify default run includes "Python" in output
- Tests verify `--all` output contains platform info
- All tests pass with `python -m pytest`

Quality Gate: python -m pytest

title: US-004: Add --help flag with usage documentation
description:
Acceptance Criteria:
- `--help` prints a usage summary listing `--version` and `--all`
- Each flag has a short description
- Handled automatically by `argparse`

Quality Gate: python -m pytest
